summary.intent: Implement CHA-16: expose additional CRM contact RPC methods (filtered list, getByExternal, getWithChannels) that were already supported by the store layer but not wired up to the RPC surface.
summary.outcome: Successfully extended the CrmService trait and LiveCrmService with 3 new/updated methods, registered 2 new RPC endpoints (crm.contacts.getByExternal, crm.contacts.getWithChannels), updated crm.contacts.list to accept filter params, added 13 new tests (26 total passing), and opened PR #13.

repo: Basic crm.contacts.* CRUD (list, get, upsert, delete) was already fully implemented before this ticket — the issue title was misleading about what remained
CrmStore trait has 21 methods but CrmService only exposed 15; the gap was list_filtered, get_by_external, get_with_channels, get_channel_by_external, list_matters_filtered, list_matters_by_contact
Auth scoping uses READ_METHODS/WRITE_METHODS string sets in crates/gateway/src/methods/mod.rs — new read methods must be added there
RPC method registration follows a consistent pattern in crates/gateway/src/methods/services.rs — each method dispatches to a CrmService trait method
NoopCrmService must be updated in service-traits whenever the CrmService trait gains new methods
macOS build environment lacks nvcc, so clippy must be run without --all-features

code.finding: CrmService trait definition — add new contact methods here; NoopCrmService follows immediately after at line 1271
All 15 CRM RPC method registrations live in this range
READ_METHODS set for CRM contacts; line 221-228 for other CRM scopes
13 existing service-level tests using MemoryCrmStore — new tests follow this pattern

workflow: CEO review and eng review phases both discovered the same gap independently — the review pipeline adds value by identifying what's already done before scoping new work
CEO review hit a blocker by asking a question (AskUserQuestion) in what was supposed to be a headless autonomous session — the skill should not pause for input
Running compile checks as background tasks while starting implementation in parallel is effective for reducing wall-clock time
Changing a zero-argument trait method signature (list_contacts → list_contacts(params: Value)) breaks existing test call sites — need to grep for all callers before committing

friction: CEO review agent incorrectly used AskUserQuestion in an autonomous/headless session, stalling the pipeline and requiring a manual 'continue' nudge
The implementation prompt had empty template fields (issue title, status, labels) suggesting a Stokowski templating bug where variables weren't substituted
Two existing tests called list_contacts() with no args and broke after the signature change — required a second fix pass
PR creation required two attempts (first gh pr create invocation failed, needed explicit --head/--base flags)
Linear workpad posting failed via GraphQL and GitHub issue API — ultimately posted to the PR instead as a fallback
open_items: crm.channels.getByExternal not wired — store supports get_channel_by_external but it's out of crm.contacts.* namespace scope
crm.matters.listFiltered and crm.matters.listByContact not wired — explicitly deferred as follow-up tickets

list_contacts with no params returns ALL contacts with no cap — potential scalability issue for large CRMs; option 6B (backward compat) was chosen, but a default limit should be added before production scale
store_err loses error type information — all CRM errors become generic string errors; structured CRM error codes would improve client-side handling
Bulk operations (batch upsert/delete), contact merge/dedup, real-time CRM event subscriptions, and fuzzy search all deferred as future features
TODOS.md had 0 items added — deferred work should likely be tracked there or in follow-up Linear tickets
